raw_text:
Approved — with two caveats to keep you from getting burned.

✅ Sign-off: Phase 17 & 18 bead set is valid

The DAG looks right, the track split is sane, and the cross-track dependencies you called out are the correct ones (notably c3→b0 and c4→a4/b4/c2). This is a clean “Truth at Scale → Production Ops/Security” progression.

Caveat 1 (must verify before burning cycles)

Your confirmation says “Priority set to P1 (highest priority)”. That’s a mismatch with your Phase 17 scope: those Track A/B/C foundations are P0 work by definition.
Action: Set priorities as:

Phase 17: bd-17a0, a1, a2, a3, a4, b0, b1, b2, b3, b4, c0, c1, c2, c3, c4 = P0

Phase 18: b0, b1, c1, d0 = P0 (security + DR + overload)

Phase 18: a0, a1, c0, e0 = P1 (release/dashboards/export)

If your system can’t represent P0, then use a “P1 = blocking” convention and mark these as blocking — but be consistent.

Caveat 2 (files_involved paths must be reality)

I can’t verify paths from here. Before assigning agents, run a quick repo check:

each file in files_involved exists or is clearly new under a real parent directory

crates referenced (platform/projections, platform/audit, platform/tenant-registry, tools/tenantctl, tools/projection-rebuild) either exist or you’ll create them as part of the foundation beads

If any of those folders don’t exist yet, that’s fine — but then the foundation beads should explicitly “create crate + Cargo.toml + wiring”.

Assignment guidance (go/no-go)

Proceed with Phase 17 execution as three parallel tracks:

Track A (Projection)

Start: bd-17a0 → a1 and a2 in parallel
Then: a3 (depends a1), a4 (depends a2)

Track B (Audit)

Start: bd-17b0 → b1, and b3 can run in parallel after b0
Then: b2 (after b1), b4 (after b1)

Track C (Tenant)

Start: bd-17c0 → (c1 and c2 in parallel)
Then: c3 (needs c1 + b0), c4 (needs a4 + b4 + c2)

My recommended first wave assignments (max throughput)

Agent 1: bd-17a0

Agent 2: bd-17b0

Agent 3: bd-17c0

Once those land, you unlock nearly everything without rework.

If you want, paste your repo tree for /platform and /tools (just ls output) and I’ll tell you immediately whether any “crate creation” beads are missing before agents start cutting code.
